evidence: Task creators repeat large, mostly-overlapping param bags and batch/single variants (`ImageGenerationTaskParams` + `BatchImageGenerationTaskParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `MagicEditTaskParams` + `BatchMagicEditTaskParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts`, wide `JoinClipsTaskParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`).

Image and video lightbox render-model hooks duplicate the same assembly flow (`useLightboxCoreSlice`/`useLightboxMediaSlice`/`useLightboxNavigationSlice` + `useLightboxWorkflowProps`) with separate large memo objects in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`.

Cross-module task handoffs rely on duplicated payload placement and fallback recovery (`topLevel`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, `individual_segment_params`) in `src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts`, indicating unclear canonical boundary ownership.

Join-clips page orchestration composes many hooks and rewraps each setting into ad-hoc inline adapters (`joinSettings.updateField(...)` repeated through large `clipSettings`/`motionConfig` objects) in `src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx`, creating seam glue entropy.

`useSegmentSlotMode` combines reducer state, router deep-link handling, structure-video overlap math, debounce/timer handling, synthetic trailing-pair reconstruction, and callback orchestration in one 587-line hook (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts`).

`MediaGallery` and `ShotImageManagerContainer` hold broad local orchestration logic in single files (655 and 412 LOC respectively), with long chains of derived state and callback plumbing that increase local cognitive load (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx`).

findings.summary: Task creation APIs duplicate broad param-bag interfaces and mapping logic across task families, increasing abstraction ceremony and drift risk.
evidence: `imageGeneration.ts` and `magicEdit.ts` each define separate single+batch interfaces with near-identical fields, then remap batch -> single manually in `buildSingleTaskParams`.
`joinClips.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts` expose very wide optional interfaces where many fields are mode-specific, but all modes share one entry contract.
findings.suggestion: Split task inputs into shared capability modules (lineage, resolution, loras, orchestration metadata) plus mode-specific discriminated unions, then reuse a common batch-to-single mapper utility.

findings.summary: Image and video lightbox render models mirror each other with high-overlap wrapper layers, paying indirection cost twice.
evidence: Both render paths build `lightboxCore`, `lightboxMedia`, `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxNavigation`, `lightboxStateValue`, and workflow props with similar memo-heavy object construction.
Layout consumption is unified (`LightboxLayout`), but upstream model assembly is duplicated in separate hook stacks.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared `useBaseLightboxRenderModel` for common assembly, with image/video specializations only for media-specific controls and edit modes.

findings.summary: Task payload fields are written in multiple locations and later recovered through fallback chains, making module boundaries and handoffs ambiguous.
evidence: `individualTravelSegment.ts` builds overlapping contracts in both `orchestrator_details` and top-level task params (`buildOrchestratorDetails` + `buildTaskParamsPayload`).
`taskParamsUtils.ts` must probe `segmentParams`, `orchestratorDetails`, `orchestratorPayload`, and top-level values (`pickScoped*` helpers), which is explicit evidence of unstable handoff location.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical serialized contract envelope per task family and enforce translation only at the persistence boundary; reduce UI readers to one typed source per field.

findings.summary: Join Clips page accumulates glue-code adapters between hooks and form sections, creating noisy seams and fragile integration points.
evidence: `JoinClipsPageLayout` constructs large `clipSettings`/`motionConfig` objects with many inline wrappers around `joinSettings.updateField`.
The page integrates many hook outputs (`useJoinClipsSettings`, `useClipManager`, `useJoinClipsGenerate`, `useProjectGenerations`) and then remaps them into multiple UI contracts in one file.
findings.suggestion: Extract a typed view-model adapter layer (`useJoinClipsPageModel`) that returns stable section DTOs and command handlers, minimizing inline boundary mapping inside JSX.

findings.summary: Large stateful hooks/components mix unrelated concerns, reducing local readability and increasing regression risk during edits.
evidence: `useSegmentSlotMode.ts` combines reducer management, route state parsing, deep-link lifecycle, overlap resolution algorithm, debounce timers, and lightbox navigation in one hook.
`MediaGallery/index.tsx` and `ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx` each coordinate many domain hooks plus extensive local derived state and callback wiring inside single modules.
findings.suggestion: Split each module into narrower units: pure data derivation helpers, route/deep-link effects, and UI action controllers; keep exported hooks/components focused on composition only.
